author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xi. 2, ‘Now when John heard in the prison the works of the Christ, he sent by his disciples, and said unto him, art thou He that cometh, or look we for another?’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xi. 2, ‘Now when John heard in the prison the works of the Christ, he sent by his disciples, and said unto him, art thou He that cometh, or look we for another?’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The lesson of the Holy Gospel has set before us a question touching John the Baptist.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May the Lord assist me to resolve it to you, as He hath resolved it to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: John was commended, as ye have heard, by the testimony of Christ, and in such terms commended, as that there had not risen a greater among those who were born of women.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But a greater than he had been born of a Virgin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much greater?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the herald himself declare, how great the difference is between himself and his Judge, whose herald he is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For John went before Christ both in his birth and preaching; but it was in obedience that he went before Him; not in preferring himself before Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so the whole train of attendants walks before the judge; yet they who walk before, are really after him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How signal a testimony then did John give to Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Even to saying that he “was not worthy to loose the latchet of His shoes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what more?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Of His fulness,” saith he, “have all we received.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He confessed that he was but a lamp lighted at His Light, and so he took refuge at His feet, lest venturing on high, he should be extinguished by the wind of pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So great indeed was he, that he was taken for Christ; and if he had not himself testified that he was not He, the mistake would have continued, and he would have been reputed to be the Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What striking humility!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Honour was proffered him by the people, and he himself refused it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Men were at fault in his greatness, and he humbled himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had no wish to increase by the words of men, seeing he had comprehended the Word of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then did John say concerning Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what said Christ of John?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have just now heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He began to say to the multitudes concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness to see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A reed shaken with the wind?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Surely not; for John was not “blown about by every wind of doctrine.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But what went ye out for to see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man clothed in soft raiment?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, for John was clothed in rough apparel; he had his raiment of camel’s hair, not of down.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But what went ye out for to see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A Prophet? yea, and more than a Prophet.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why “more than a Prophet”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Prophets foretold that the Lord would come, whom they desired to see, and saw not; but to him was vouchsafed what they sought.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: John saw the Lord; he saw Him, pointed his finger toward Him, and said, “Behold the Lamb of God, who taketh away the sins of the world;” behold, here He is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now had He come and was not acknowledged; and so a mistake was made also as to John himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold then here is He whom the Patriarchs desired to see, whom the Prophets foretold, whom the Law prefigured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Behold the Lamb of God, who taketh away the sins of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he gave a goodly testimony to the Lord, and the Lord to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Among them that are born of women,” saith the Lord, “there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding, he that is less in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he;” less in time, but greater in majesty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This He said, meaning Himself to be understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now exceedingly great among men is John the Baptist, than whom among men Christ alone is greater.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It may also be thus stated and explained, “Among them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding, he that is the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not in the sense that I have before explained it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Notwithstanding, he that is the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he;” the kingdom of heaven he meant where the Angels are; he then that is the least among the Angels, is greater than John.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus He set forth to us the excellence of that kingdom which we should long for; set before us a city, of which we should desire to be citizens.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What sort of citizens are there? how great are they!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso is the least there, is greater than John.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Than what John?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Than whom there hath not risen a greater among them that are born of women.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus have we heard the true and good record both of John concerning Christ, and of Christ concerning John.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is the meaning of this; that John sent his disciples to Him when He was shut up in prison, on the eve of being put to death, and said to them, “Go, say to Him, Art Thou He that should come, or do we look for another?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is this then all that praise?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That praise is it turned to doubting?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What sayest thou, John.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To Whom art thou speaking?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What sayest thou?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou speakest to thy Judge, thyself the herald.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou stretchedst out the finger, and pointedst Him out; thou saidst, “Behold the Lamb of God, behold Him who taketh away the sins of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou saidst, “Of His fulness have we all received.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou saidst, “I am not worthy to unloose the latchet of His shoes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And dost thou now say, “Art Thou He that should come, or do we look for another?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is not this the same Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who art thou?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Art thou not His forerunner?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Art thou not he of whom it was foretold, “Behold, I send my messenger before Thy face, who shall prepare Thy way before thee?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How dost thou prepare the way, and thou art thyself straying from the way?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the disciples of John came; and the Lord said to them, “Go, tell John, the blind see, the deaf hear, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the poor have the Gospel preached to them; and blessed is he whosoever shall not be offended in Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not suspect that John was offended in Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet his words do sound so; “Art Thou He that should come?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask my works; “The blind see, the deaf hear, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the dead are raised, the poor have the Gospel preached to them;” and dost thou ask whether I am He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My works, saith He, are My words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Go, show him again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And as they departed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lest haply any one should say, John was good at first, and the Spirit of God forsook him; therefore after their departure, he spake these words; after their departure whom John had sent, Christ commended John.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the meaning then of this obscure question?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May that Sun shine upon us, from which that lamp derived its flame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so the resolution of it is altogether plain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: John had separate disciples of his own; not as in separation from Christ, but prepared as a witness to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For meet it was that such an one should give his testimony to Christ, who was himself also gathering disciples, and who might have been envious of Him, for that he could not see Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore because John’s disciples highly esteemed their master, they heard from John his record concerning Christ, and marvelled; and as he was about to die, it was his wish that they should be confirmed by him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For no doubt they were saying among themselves; Such great things doth he say of Him, but none such of himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Go then, ask Him;” not because I doubt, but that ye may be instructed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Go, ask Him,” hear from Himself what I am in the habit of telling you; ye have heard the herald, be confirmed by the Judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Go, ask Him, Art Thou He that should come, or do we look for another?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They went accordingly and asked; not for John’s sake, but for their own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And for their sakes did Christ say, “The blind see, the lame walk, the deaf hear, the lepers are cleansed, the dead are raised, the poor have the Gospel preached to them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye see Me, acknowledge Me then; ye see the works, acknowledge the Doer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And blessed is he whosoever shall not be offended in Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But it is of you I speak, not of John.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that we might know that He spake not this of John, as they departed, “He began to speak to the multitudes concerning John;” the True, the Truth Himself, proclaimed his true praises.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I think this question has been sufficiently explained.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let it suffice then to have prolonged my address thus far.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now keep the poor in mind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give, ye who have not given hitherto; believe me, ye will not lose it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, truly, that only it seems ye lose, which ye do not carry to the circus.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now must we render unto the poor the offerings of such of you as have offered anything, and the amount which we have is much less than your usual offerings.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shake off this sloth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am become a beggar for beggars; what is that to me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would be a beggar for beggars, that ye may be reckoned among the number of children.
